executiveSummary.text: US risk tone was mixed, not broken: S&P 500 -0.28%, Nasdaq +0.20%, Dow -1.09%, VIX +4.77% to 16.90. Mega-cap AI offset oil/rates pressure while cyclicals lagged.
Rates stayed the regime constraint: 5Y 4.31%, 10Y 4.57%, 30Y 5.07%, DXY 101.07 (-0.07%). Fresh Fed minutes showed officials split, including a few seeing a June hike case.

Oil was the cleanest macro shock: WTI +5.81% to $74.53, Brent +6.57% to $79.03 after fresh Iran/Hormuz escalation headlines. Transmission path is oil -> inflation expectations -> term premium -> tech multiples.
AI/semis leadership reasserted selectively: AVGO +4.83% on Apple’s >$30B US chip deal; NVDA +3.65%; AMAT/KLAC/LRCX also firm. Samsung-related AI-expectation worries remain the counter-signal.
Scarcity sleeve was mixed: silver -3.62%, SLV -2.99%, COPX -2.13%, gold -1.45%; uranium held better with URNM +1.32%. This is more real-rate/USD/liquidity repricing than thesis damage.
Singapore/Asia lens: STI last print +1.57%; UOB +3.93% stood out, while S-REIT commentary turned constructive as easing inflation/rate hopes improve rerating odds.

usGlobal.what: Index tape was divergent: Nasdaq up, S&P slightly down, Dow/Russell weaker, VIX higher from low levels.
usGlobal.why: AI winners absorbed capital while oil and yields pressured cyclicals/rate-duration equities.
usGlobal.implication: Risk appetite exists, but breadth is not confirming a broad melt-up.

ratesMacro.what: Fed minutes showed a divided committee; a few officials saw a June hike case.
ratesMacro.why: Oil shocks and sticky inflation keep the Fed from validating aggressive easing.
ratesMacro.implication: Duration and high-multiple tech remain vulnerable if energy feeds inflation expectations.

asiaSingapore.what: STI was firm and Singapore banks remained resilient; UOB was an abnormal positive mover.
asiaSingapore.why: Banks benefit from higher-for-longer net interest income while local REIT rerating hopes depend on yields stabilising.
asiaSingapore.implication: SG dividend sleeve remains ballast; S-REIT upside needs rates to stop rising.

commodities.why: Geopolitical supply risk lifted crude, while higher real/nominal yields and liquidity pressure weighed on silver/copper miners.
commodities.implication: Energy inflation is the macro risk; metals weakness is not automatically bearish unless copper demand or grid capex rolls over.

geopolitics.why: The path is shipping risk and crude supply premium, not generic safe-haven fear.
geopolitics.implication: Sustained Brent above the mid/high-$80s would raise inflation-risk pricing and pressure long-duration equities.

moves.why: Fresh reports said Apple committed more than $30B to Broadcom for US chipmaking, reinforcing custom silicon/networking demand.
moves.implication: Supports KL’s AI infrastructure/toll-road thesis; 2nd order: validates ASIC and advanced connectivity suppliers beyond NVDA.
moves.action: HOLD / ADD only on broad semicap pullbacks; invalidator is order quality shifting from durable Apple custom silicon to one-off politically driven capex.

moves.what: NVDA rose +3.65% despite a mixed chip tape.
moves.why: Options/positioning and renewed AI leadership demand overcame oil/rates pressure.
moves.implication: Underweight remains deliberate; do not chase if portfolio already has broad AI exposure through AVGO, TSM, equipment, QQQ.
moves.action: HOLD / IGNORE chase; add only after earnings-backed estimate revisions, not momentum alone.

moves.why: Singapore bank strength fits higher-for-longer rate and dividend-resilience positioning; no single fresh fundamental break found in 24h scan.
moves.implication: SG dividend sleeve continues to be ballast against global tech volatility.
moves.action: HOLD; trim only if bank sleeve becomes oversized or NIM/credit-cycle data deteriorates.

moves.what: SLV fell -2.99% and silver futures -3.62%; COPX fell -2.13%.
moves.why: Metals sold off even as oil rose, consistent with real-rate/liquidity pressure and profit-taking, not a clean scarcity-thesis failure.
moves.implication: 3rd order: if oil keeps yields high, energy scarcity can temporarily crowd out metals scarcity via tighter financial conditions.
moves.action: WATCH / ADD in tranches only if copper demand indicators and grid capex remain intact while price corrects further.

moves.what: SPGI fell -2.86%, near but below the abnormal threshold.
moves.why: Likely factor/rates pressure on financial-data compounders; no fresh thesis-breaking headline found.
moves.implication: Financial toll-road thesis intact unless debt issuance, ratings volumes, or index licensing growth weaken.

thesisImpact.what: Scarcity thesis remains intact but path is bumpier.
thesisImpact.why: AI demand signals improved for AVGO/NVDA while oil shock threatens discount rates.
thesisImpact.implication: Best risk/reward is still staged accumulation on forced macro pullbacks, not chasing single-day spikes.
thesisImpact.action: Keep cash optionality; deploy only at pre-set valuation/technical triggers.

narratives.title: AI capex broadens from GPUs to custom silicon and connectivity
narratives.description: Apple-Broadcom deal supports the view that AI infrastructure value accrues to ASICs, networking, EDA, foundry and equipment, not only GPU leaders.
narratives.priceImpact: Positive for AVGO and AI supply-chain breadth; raises valuation discipline needs.

narratives.title: Oil shock challenges the soft-landing multiple
narratives.description: Fresh Iran/Hormuz escalation lifts crude and can reprice inflation expectations and term premium if sustained.
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for duration equities if Brent remains elevated.

narratives.title: S-REIT rerating is conditional, not automatic
narratives.description: Fresh Singapore coverage says easing inflation fears brighten S-REIT outlook, but hawkish Fed/yield pressure remains the gatekeeper.
narratives.source: Singapore Business Review / Business Times last 24h headlines
narratives.priceImpact: Constructive only if long yields stabilize or fall.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil up -> inflation expectations/term premium up -> Fed cuts priced out -> AI multiples compress even if AI revenues are fine.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Separate thesis quality from discount-rate volatility; use cash ballast to buy forced deratings.

secondOrderEffects.text: Apple-Broadcom custom silicon deal -> more diversified AI silicon stack -> foundry/equipment/EDA demand durability -> KL benefits via AVGO/TSM/ASML/LRCX/AMAT/KLAC/SNPS.
secondOrderEffects.implication: NVDA underweight is less risky when AI exposure is diversified across bottlenecks.

secondOrderEffects.text: Higher-for-longer rates -> Singapore banks supported via NIM -> S-REITs capped by discount rates.
secondOrderEffects.implication: SG dividend sleeve can self-hedge, but REIT additions should wait for yield confirmation.

investment.thesis: Accumulate AI bottleneck basket on broad macro selloffs, prioritising foundry/equipment/EDA/networking over single-name GPU chase.
investment.trigger: QQQ/semis down 7-10% without earnings revisions collapsing; or AVGO/TSM/ASML/LRCX/AMAT/KLAC/SNPS reset to target valuation bands.
investment.whyNow: Fresh AVGO news confirms AI value-chain broadening, while oil/rates may create entry volatility.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, foundry utilization downturn, or export controls that impair earnings power.

investment.thesis: Keep cash/CPF/property ballast intact; dry powder has option value in a high-yield, geopolitically volatile regime.
investment.trigger: Deploy only into pre-committed drawdown levels or thesis-confirming earnings.
investment.whyNow: 10Y/30Y yields remain high and oil shock can create nonlinear de-risking.
investment.invalidator: Clear Fed easing cycle plus broad earnings acceleration that lowers cash hurdle.

trading.thesis: Do not chase oil-spike beneficiaries after a one-day crude surge; wait for confirmation.
trading.trigger: Brent sustains above $85-90 with shipping insurance/freight stress and backwardation widening.
trading.whyNow: Headline risk is high but reversals are common if geopolitical escalation fades.
trading.invalidator: Ceasefire credibility improves or crude gives back the spike.

trading.thesis: Use metals weakness as a watch, not an automatic buy.
trading.trigger: Copper holds key support while China/grid/AI power data remain firm; silver stabilizes after volatility compression.
trading.whyNow: SLV/COPX crossed abnormal-move thresholds, but the driver looks macro/liquidity rather than thesis failure.
trading.invalidator: Copper demand indicators weaken or real yields break higher.

watchlist.thesis: Fed/oil interaction is the main regime trigger.
watchlist.trigger: Brent above high-$80s plus 10Y above 4.75% and VIX above 20.
watchlist.whyNow: That combination would convert an energy shock into a discount-rate shock.

watchlist.trigger: US/Singapore long yields roll over and REIT prices break above recent ranges on volume.
watchlist.whyNow: Fresh local commentary is constructive but not yet enough.
watchlist.invalidator: Fed stays hawkish and financing costs rise.

riskWatch.name: Oil-to-rates shock
riskWatch.description: Iran/Hormuz escalation can lift crude, inflation expectations and term premium, pressuring long-duration tech.
riskWatch.mitigation: Avoid leverage; keep cash; stage entries.

riskWatch.name: AI valuation concentration
riskWatch.description: Leadership remains narrow; Samsung-related AI expectation worries show how high the bar is.
riskWatch.mitigation: Prefer diversified bottleneck basket and earnings discipline.

riskWatch.name: Singapore REIT duration risk
riskWatch.description: Rerating needs lower/stable yields; higher-for-longer caps NAV multiples.
riskWatch.mitigation: Add only after yield confirmation; maintain banks as ballast.

keyEvents.text: Fed minutes: officials split; a few saw case for June hike.
Apple commits more than $30B to Broadcom for US chipmaking.
Oil jumped on renewed Iran/Hormuz escalation headlines.
S-REITs highlighted as potential rerating candidates if inflation/rates ease.
keyEvents.source: Singapore Business Review / Business Times last 24h

bottomLine.whatMatters: Whether the oil spike becomes an inflation/rates shock, and whether AI capex breadth keeps earnings revisions positive. Scenario weights: 55% choppy risk-on with narrow AI leadership; 25% oil/rates shock causes 5-10% equity pullback; 15% broader catch-up if yields fall; 5% disorderly geopolitical/liquidity event. Confidence: medium.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core VWRA/AI/SG dividend barbell; do not chase NVDA/AVGO single-day strength; prepare staged adds in AI bottlenecks and scarcity assets only on macro-driven drawdowns with thesis intact.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: One-day commodity safe-haven narratives and portfolio-level noise that is only discount-rate repricing, not earnings-power damage.
